hypothesis: On US500 M15, when higher-timeframe trend is up (H1 EMA50 > EMA200 and price > EMA200) a shallow pullback to the M15 EMA20 with RSI(14) dipping to 40-50 and recovering offers asymmetric continuation. Symmetric short logic in H1 downtrends. This exploits the post-2020 US500 buy-the-dip regime during US cash session.

entry_rules.long: H1_ema50 > H1_ema200 AND H1_close > H1_ema200 AND low <= ema20 AND close > ema20 AND rsi14 between 40 and 58 AND rsi14 > rsi14[1] AND session_mask==1
entry_rules.short: H1_ema50 < H1_ema200 AND H1_close < H1_ema200 AND high >= ema20 AND close < ema20 AND rsi14 between 42 and 60 AND rsi14 < rsi14[1] AND session_mask==1

regime_filter.rule: adx(14, H1) >= 18 AND atr_percentile(14, 200) between 20 and 90 AND classify in ['trend', 'weak_trend']
regime_filter.rationale: Need directional regime for pullback continuation; exclude chop and vol-crisis tape.

notes: Diversifier: trend-following family on US500 to balance the two mean-reversion specs and one breakout spec. Avoids prior dead branches (not Donchian, not pure breakout on index).
